metadata.name: bcbio-gff
metadata.summary: Read and write Generic Feature Format (GFF) with Biopython integration.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF._utils.defaultdict
documentation: Back compatible defaultdict: http://code.activestate.com/recipes/523034/

modules.name: BCBio.GFF
documentation: Top level of GFF parsing providing shortcuts for useful classes.

modules.name: BCBio
documentation: BCBio module

modules.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput
documentation:
Output Biopython SeqRecords and SeqFeatures to GFF3 format.

The target format is GFF3, the current GFF standard:
    http://www.sequenceontology.org/gff3.shtml

functions.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput.write
documentation:
High level interface to write GFF3 files from SeqRecords and SeqFeatures.

If include_fasta is True, the GFF3 file will include sequence information
using the ##FASTA directive.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput._IdHandler

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput._IdHandler._generate_id
documentation: Generate a unique ID not present in our existing IDs.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput._IdHandler._get_standard_id
documentation:
Retrieve standardized IDs from other sources like NCBI GenBank.

This tries to find IDs from known key/values when stored differently
than GFF3 specifications.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput._IdHandler.update_quals
documentation: Update a set of qualifiers, adding an ID if necessary.
Generate IDs for GFF3 Parent/Child relationships where they don't exist.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput.GFF3Writer

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput.GFF3Writer.write
documentation: Write the provided records to the given handle in GFF3 format.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput.GFF3Writer._write_feature
documentation: Write a feature with location information.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput.GFF3Writer._write_annotations
documentation: Add annotations which refer to an entire sequence.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput.GFF3Writer._write_header
documentation: Write out standard header directives.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFOutput.GFF3Writer._write_fasta
documentation: Write sequence records using the ##FASTA directive.
Write GFF3 files starting with standard Biopython objects.

modules.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser
documentation:
Parse GFF files into features attached to Biopython SeqRecord objects.

This deals with GFF3 formatted files, a tab delimited format for storing
sequence features and annotations:

http://www.sequenceontology.org/gff3.shtml

It will also deal with older GFF versions (GTF/GFF2):

http://www.sanger.ac.uk/Software/formats/GFF/GFF_Spec.shtml
http://mblab.wustl.edu/GTF22.html

The implementation utilizes map/reduce parsing of GFF using Disco. Disco
(http://discoproject.org) is a Map-Reduce framework for Python utilizing
Erlang for parallelization. The code works on a single processor without
Disco using the same architecture.

functions.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._gff_line_map
documentation:
Map part of Map-Reduce; parses a line of GFF into a dictionary.

Given an input line from a GFF file, this:
- decides if the file passes our filtering limits
- if so:
    - breaks it into component elements
    - determines the type of attribute (flat, parent, child or annotation)
    - generates a dictionary of GFF info which can be serialized as JSON

functions.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._gff_line_reduce
documentation: Reduce part of Map-Reduce; combines results of parsed features.

functions.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.parse
documentation: High level interface to parse GFF files into SeqRecords and SeqFeatures.

functions.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.parse_simple
documentation: Parse GFF files as line by line dictionary of parts.

functions.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._file_or_handle
documentation: Decorator to handle either an input handle or a file.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._MultiIDRemapper
documentation:
Provide an ID remapping for cases where a parent has a non-unique ID.

Real life GFF3 cases have non-unique ID attributes, which we fix here
by using the unique sequence region to assign children to the right
parent.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF.__init__
documentation:
Initialize GFF parser

create_missing - If True, create blank records for GFF ids not in
the base_dict. If False, an error will be raised.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF.parse
documentation:
Parse a GFF file, returning an iterator of SeqRecords.

limit_info - A dictionary specifying the regions of the GFF file
which should be extracted. This allows only relevant portions of a file
to be parsed.

base_dict - A base dictionary of SeqRecord objects which may be
pre-populated with sequences and other features. The new features from
the GFF file will be added to this dictionary.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF.parse_in_parts
documentation:
Parse a region of a GFF file specified, returning info as generated.

target_lines -- The number of lines in the file which should be used
for each partial parse. This should be determined based on available
memory.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF.parse_simple
documentation:
Simple parse which does not build or nest features.

This returns a simple dictionary representation of each line in the
GFF file.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._normalize_limit_info
documentation: Turn all limit information into tuples for identical comparisons.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._results_to_features
documentation: Add parsed dictionaries of results to Biopython SeqFeatures.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_directives
documentation:
Handle any directives or meta-data in the GFF file.

Relevant items are added as annotation meta-data to each record.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._get_matching_record_id
documentation:
Find a matching base record with the test identifier, handling tricky cases.

NCBI IDs https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FASTA_format#NCBI_identifiers

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_seqs
documentation: Add sequence information contained in the GFF3 to records.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_parent_child_features
documentation: Add nested features with parent child relationships.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._identify_dup_ids
documentation:
Identify duplicated ID attributes in potential nested parents.

According to the GFF3 spec ID attributes are supposed to be unique
for a file, but this is not always true in practice. This looks
for duplicates, and provides unique IDs sorted by locations.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_children_to_parent
documentation: Recursively add children to parent features.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_annotations
documentation: Add annotation data from the GFF file to records.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_ann_to_rec
documentation: Add a key/value annotation to the given SeqRecord.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._get_rec
documentation: Retrieve a record to add features to.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_missing_parent
documentation: Add a new feature that is missing from the GFF file.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._add_toplevel_feature
documentation: Add a toplevel non-nested feature to the appropriate record.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._get_feature
documentation: Retrieve a Biopython feature from our dictionary representation.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._AbstractMapReduceGFF._parse_fasta
documentation: Parse FASTA sequence information contained in the GFF3 file.

Base class providing general GFF parsing for local and remote classes.

This class should be subclassed to provide a concrete class to parse
GFF under specific conditions. These classes need to implement
the _gff_process function, which returns a dictionary of SeqRecord
information.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser._GFFParserLocalOut
documentation: Provide a collector for local GFF MapReduce file parsing.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.GFFParser

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.GFFParser._gff_process
documentation:
Process GFF addition without any parallelization.

In addition to limit filtering, this accepts a target_lines attribute
which provides a number of lines to parse before returning results.
This allows partial parsing of a file to prevent memory issues.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.GFFParser._file_line_generator
documentation: Generate single lines from a set of GFF files.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.GFFParser._lines_to_out_info
documentation: Generate SeqRecord and SeqFeatures from GFF file lines.
Local GFF parser providing standardized parsing of GFF3 and GFF2 files.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.DiscoGFFParser

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.DiscoGFFParser.__init__
documentation:
Initialize parser.

disco_host - Web reference to a Disco host which will be used for
parallelizing the GFF reading job.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.DiscoGFFParser._gff_process
documentation: Process GFF addition, using Disco to parallelize the process.
GFF Parser with parallelization through Disco (http://discoproject.org.

classes.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.GFFExaminer

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.GFFExaminer.available_limits
documentation:
Return dictionary information on possible limits for this file.

This returns a nested dictionary with the following structure:

keys -- names of items to filter by
values -- dictionary with:
    keys -- filter choice
    value -- counts of that filter in this file

Not a parallelized map-reduce implementation.

methods.name: BCBio.GFF.GFFParser.GFFExaminer.parent_child_map
documentation:
Provide a mapping of parent to child relationships in the file.

Returns a dictionary of parent child relationships:

keys -- tuple of (source, type) for each parent
values -- tuple of (source, type) as children of that parent

Not a parallelized map-reduce implementation.

Provide high level details about a GFF file to refine parsing.

GFF is a spec and is provided by many different centers. Real life files
will present the same information in slightly different ways. Becoming
familiar with the file you are dealing with is the best way to extract the
information you need. This class provides high level summary details to
help in learning.
